guild_type.brief: Represents the configuration and progression parameters for a character guild or class in the game.
guild_type.details: The `guild_type` struct defines the core attributes, skill groups, and leveling mechanics associated with a specific guild or character class. It includes identifiers such as `name` for the full descriptive name and `who_name` for a concise three-letter code used for quick reference. The struct specifies the primary attribute (`stat_prime`) that influences the guild's abilities, along with the initial weapon (`weapon`) assigned to members. It also sets the maximum skill level (`skill_adept`) attainable within the guild. Combat effectiveness at different levels is modeled through `thac0_00` and `thac0_32`, representing attack thresholds at levels 0 and 32 respectively. Skill progression is managed via `base_group` and `default_group`, which define the starting and default skill categories. Additionally, the struct models resource gains upon leveling, including health points (`hp_min`, `hp_max`), mana (`mana_min`, `mana_max`), and stamina (`stam_min`, `stam_max`), with minimum and maximum bounds to ensure balanced growth. This comprehensive structure enables flexible configuration of guilds, supporting gameplay mechanics, character progression, and balancing within the game system.

tparams.name: The full, human-readable name of the guild or character class.
tparams.who_name: A three-letter identifier for quick reference to the guild.
tparams.stat_prime: The primary attribute that influences the guild's abilities and performance.
tparams.weapon: Identifier for the guild's starting or primary weapon.
tparams.skill_adept: Maximum skill level that members of the guild can attain.
tparams.thac0_00: Thac0 value used for combat calculations at level 0.
tparams.thac0_32: Thac0 value used for combat calculations at level 32.
tparams.base_group: The set of skills designated as the core or base skills for the guild.
tparams.default_group: The default skill category or group assigned to the guild.
tparams.hp_min: Minimum health points gained when leveling up.
tparams.hp_max: Maximum health points gained when leveling up.
tparams.mana_min: Minimum mana points gained upon leveling.
tparams.mana_max: Maximum mana points gained upon leveling.
tparams.stam_min: Minimum stamina points gained upon leveling.
tparams.stam_max: Maximum stamina points gained upon leveling.

guild_type.notes: This struct employs a straightforward data-driven approach to define guild characteristics, facilitating easy adjustments and balancing. The use of min/max bounds for resource gains ensures predictable and controlled progression, while the inclusion of combat parameters like Thac0 at specific levels supports nuanced combat mechanics. The design supports extensibility for additional attributes or mechanics if needed.
guild_type.rationale: The `guild_type` struct exists to encapsulate all relevant data for defining character classes within the game, enabling flexible configuration and consistent behavior across different guilds. It serves as a foundational component in the character progression system, allowing developers and designers to fine-tune class attributes, skills, and growth patterns systematically, thereby supporting balanced gameplay and diverse class identities within the system architecture.
guild_type.name: guild_type

name.brief: Holds the full, human-readable name of the guild type.
name.details: The `name` variable stores the complete name of the guild type, which is used for display purposes and identification within the system. It is initialized with a descriptive string representing the guild type and can be accessed or modified as needed throughout the codebase. The use of the custom String class allows for advanced string operations, such as case-insensitive comparisons, facilitating flexible name matching and user interface display logic.
name.notes: Utilizes a specialized String class that extends standard string functionality, enabling more robust string handling and comparison features.
name.rationale: This variable exists to provide a clear, human-readable identifier for each guild type, supporting display, search, and comparison functionalities within the application.
name.name: name

who_name.brief: A three-letter string identifier representing the 'who' entity associated with this guild.
who_name.details: The 'who_name' variable holds a concise, three-character string that uniquely identifies the 'who' entity within the context of the guild. It is typically initialized with a specific code or name during object setup and is used for quick reference, display, or comparison operations. By utilizing the custom String class, which offers enhanced string manipulation features such as case-insensitive comparisons, 'who_name' supports flexible matching and processing of 'who' identifiers throughout the codebase, ensuring efficient and reliable identification of related entities.
who_name.notes: The variable's fixed length of three characters makes it suitable for compact storage and fast comparisons, especially when used with the String class's optimized comparison methods.
who_name.rationale: This variable exists to provide a standardized, easily comparable identifier for the 'who' entity associated with the guild, enabling consistent referencing and processing within various system components.
who_name.name: who_name

stat_prime.brief: Represents the primary attribute associated with a guild type.
stat_prime.details: The `stat_prime` variable stores the main attribute that defines the core characteristic of a guild type, such as strength, intelligence, or agility. It is initialized according to the specific configuration of the guild and is used in various calculations and decision-making processes that determine the guild's behavior, effectiveness, and interactions within the system. This attribute helps differentiate guild types and influences their operational roles.
stat_prime.notes: Typically, `stat_prime` is set during guild creation and remains constant unless explicitly modified. It plays a key role in attribute-based mechanics and may be referenced in multiple parts of the codebase to assess guild capabilities.
stat_prime.rationale: This variable exists to encapsulate the primary attribute that defines each guild type, enabling consistent and efficient access to this core characteristic for gameplay mechanics and system logic.
stat_prime.name: stat_prime

weapon.brief: Stores the identifier of the guild's primary weapon.
weapon.details: The `weapon` variable holds the ID corresponding to the guild's main weapon, used in combat scenarios and equipment management. It is initialized during the guild's creation or setup phase and can be updated if the guild's equipment changes. This variable links to predefined weapon type constants or IDs used consistently across the codebase to facilitate weapon handling and referencing.
weapon.notes: Typically stored as an integer ID, which allows for efficient lookups and comparisons. Proper management ensures the guild's weapon state remains consistent with game logic.
weapon.rationale: This variable exists to track the guild's primary weapon, enabling the game to determine combat capabilities and display relevant equipment information based on the assigned weapon ID.
weapon.name: weapon

skill_adept.brief: Stores the maximum skill level a character can attain for a specific skill type.
skill_adept.details: The `skill_adept` variable defines the upper limit for a character's proficiency in a particular skill category, such as combat or crafting. It is initialized based on character attributes, class, or game progression, and is used throughout the game logic to cap skill advancement. This variable interacts with skill check routines and progression systems to prevent characters from exceeding their designated skill cap, thereby maintaining balanced gameplay and character development constraints.
skill_adept.notes: Typically stored as an integer value, `skill_adept` is updated during character progression or class changes to reflect new skill caps. It is often used in conjunction with current skill levels to determine eligibility for advanced skills or abilities.
skill_adept.rationale: This variable exists to enforce skill level boundaries, ensuring characters develop within intended limits and supporting consistent gameplay mechanics related to skill progression.
skill_adept.name: skill_adept

thac0_00.brief: The Thac0 value used for characters at level 0 in combat calculations.
thac0_00.details: This variable holds the Thac0 (To Hit Armor Class 0) value specific to level 0 characters, serving as the baseline for attack success calculations at the starting level. It is initialized to a default value that reflects the base attack threshold for level 0 entities and is referenced during combat to determine whether an attack hits or misses. The value may be adjusted based on character attributes or game mechanics but primarily represents the initial attack capability for newly created or level 0 characters.
thac0_00.notes: Typically, this variable is stored as an integer and remains constant unless explicitly modified during gameplay or character progression. It is part of the guild_type structure, indicating that each guild or class may have its own Thac0 baseline for level 0 characters.
thac0_00.rationale: This variable exists to define the initial attack threshold for level 0 characters, enabling consistent and accurate combat calculations at the starting point of character development.
thac0_00.name: thac0_00

thac0_32.brief: The Thac0 value used for level 32 characters of this guild or class.
thac0_32.details: This variable holds the Thac0 (To Hit Armor Class 0) value specific to level 32, which is used in combat calculations to determine the success of attack rolls against opponents' armor classes. It is initialized based on the character's class progression and is referenced during attack resolution to compute whether an attack hits or misses. As part of the guild_type enumeration, it allows different character classes or guilds to have distinct combat parameters tailored to their progression at level 32.
thac0_32.notes: Typically, these Thac0 values are stored for each level and class combination to facilitate quick access during combat computations, ensuring efficient hit chance calculations.
thac0_32.rationale: This variable exists to provide a specific combat parameter for level 32 characters, enabling accurate and class-specific attack calculations that reflect the character's progression and combat effectiveness at this level.
thac0_32.name: thac0_32

base_group.brief: A string that specifies the core skill group associated with the guild type.
base_group.details: The 'base_group' variable holds the name of the fundamental skill category linked to the guild type, typically initialized with a descriptive string during guild setup. It serves as a key identifier for the primary skills members of this guild will acquire or develop, influencing skill progression and access to related abilities. This variable interacts with skill management and progression systems, ensuring that the core skills are consistently referenced and applied across the game mechanics.
base_group.notes: Utilizes the custom String class, which supports case-insensitive comparisons and advanced string manipulation, facilitating flexible handling of skill group names.
base_group.rationale: This variable exists to define and reference the primary skill set associated with each guild type, supporting consistent skill assignment and progression logic within the game.
base_group.name: base_group

default_group.brief: Represents the default skill group or category associated with the guild.
default_group.details: The `guild_type::default_group` variable stores the name of the default skill group that is assigned to the guild's members. It is initialized with a string indicating this category, which is used during skill assignment processes to categorize or group skills appropriately. This variable interacts with skill management functions to ensure that new skills are automatically associated with the designated default group, facilitating organized skill progression and categorization within the guild.
default_group.notes: Utilizes the custom `String` class for flexible string handling, including case-insensitive comparisons, which can be useful when matching or displaying group names.
default_group.rationale: This variable exists to define a default categorization for skills gained by the guild, supporting consistent skill grouping and simplifying skill assignment logic across the guild's operations.
default_group.name: default_group

hp_min.brief: The minimum health points (HP) gained by a character when leveling up.
hp_min.details: This variable represents the lower limit of health points a character can gain during the level-up process. It is initialized based on character class or type and ensures that the character receives at least this amount of HP increase regardless of other modifiers. The value influences character survivability by guaranteeing a minimum health gain, and it interacts with other HP-related variables to regulate growth and progression mechanics.
hp_min.notes: Typically stored as part of character progression data, this variable helps maintain consistent minimum health gains across different character types and level-up scenarios.
hp_min.rationale: The variable exists to enforce a baseline health increase during leveling, supporting balanced character growth and preventing excessively low HP gains from random or conditional effects.
hp_min.name: hp_min

hp_max.brief: The maximum hit points (HP) that a guild type can gain upon leveling.
hp_max.details: This variable defines the upper limit of hit points that a guild type can acquire when it levels up. It is initialized according to the guild's design parameters and serves as a cap to prevent exceeding the intended maximum HP gain during progression. The hp_max value influences the durability and survivability of entities associated with this guild type, ensuring they remain within balanced health thresholds throughout gameplay.
hp_max.notes: Typically stored as an integer, this variable is used during level-up calculations to restrict HP increases, maintaining game balance and consistency.
hp_max.rationale: The hp_max variable exists to enforce a maximum HP gain per level, supporting balanced growth and preventing overpowered increases in entity durability within the game mechanics.
hp_max.name: hp_max

mana_min.brief: The minimum amount of mana gained by a character when leveling up.
mana_min.details: This variable represents the lowest possible mana increase a character can receive upon leveling up, ensuring a baseline resource gain. It is initialized according to character class, configuration settings, or game balance parameters, and is used during the level-up process to calculate mana progression. The value can be modified by game mechanics or modifiers that affect resource growth, maintaining balanced and predictable mana development across different character types.
mana_min.notes: Typically stored as an integer, this variable ensures that characters always gain at least a minimal amount of mana when leveling, preventing zero or negative gains. It may be adjusted dynamically based on in-game effects or character attributes.
mana_min.rationale: This variable exists to enforce a minimum mana gain during level-up events, supporting consistent resource progression and preventing scenarios where characters might receive no mana increase, which could hinder gameplay balance.
mana_min.name: mana_min

mana_max.brief: The maximum amount of mana a guild can gain upon leveling.
mana_max.details: This variable represents the upper limit of mana points that a guild can acquire when it levels up. It is initialized based on the guild's current level and is updated accordingly during level progression. The value ensures that the guild's mana resource does not exceed this cap, maintaining balanced resource growth. It plays a crucial role in level-up calculations and resource management, reflecting the guild's capacity for mana accumulation as it advances.
mana_max.notes: Typically set during guild initialization and updated on level-up events to reflect the guild's growth.
mana_max.rationale: This variable exists to enforce a cap on mana gains, preventing resource overflow and supporting consistent progression mechanics within the game.
mana_max.name: mana_max

stam_min.brief: The minimum stamina points awarded to a character upon leveling up.
stam_min.details: This variable represents the baseline amount of stamina a character gains when they level up, ensuring a consistent minimum resource increase. It is initialized according to character class, configuration settings, or game balance parameters, and may be modified by in-game effects or buffs. The value helps maintain predictable progression and resource management for players.
stam_min.notes: Typically stored as part of the character's guild or class data, this value is crucial for balancing stamina growth and ensuring fair resource distribution during level-ups.
stam_min.rationale: The variable exists to define a guaranteed minimum stamina increase, supporting balanced character progression and resource planning within the game mechanics.
stam_min.name: stam_min

stam_max.brief: The maximum stamina a character can have after leveling up.
stam_max.details: This variable represents the upper limit of stamina points a character can possess, which is set upon leveling and may be influenced by character class, level, and other modifiers. It is used to cap the stamina resource during gameplay, ensuring characters do not exceed this maximum. The value is initialized during character creation and can be adjusted through game events, items, or buffs that modify stamina capacity.
stam_max.notes: Typically stored as an integer and updated only during level-ups or stamina capacity modifications to maintain consistency.
stam_max.rationale: This variable exists to enforce a cap on stamina, preventing characters from exceeding their designed maximum, and to facilitate gameplay balance by controlling stamina growth and resource management.
stam_max.name: stam_max
